Marketing

How to Market Your SaaS for Free: 14 Platforms to Explode Your Growth in 2026

Smart Link Team
April 20, 202612 min

Let's be honest: Most developers hate marketing. We'd rather spend 12 hours debugging a race condition than 15 minutes trying to write a catchy tweet. But if you've built a product as powerful as Smart Link, it deserves to be seen. The good news? You don't need to be a "social media influencer" or show your face on camera to get your first 1,000 users.

The "Silent Marketer" Strategy

In 2026, the best marketing for technical products isn't about being loud; it's about being useful. Instead of fighting for attention on platforms you don't use, focus on "Passive Acquisition"—putting your product where people are already looking for solutions.

14 Free Platforms Every SaaS Founder Should Use

These platforms are designed for discovery. You list your product once, and they continue to drive traffic for months.

The Big Three

  • Product Hunt: The classic launchpad. Perfect for a 24-hour traffic spike.
  • Indie Hackers: A community of builders. Share your journey and get high-quality feedback.
  • Hacker News: high-risk, high-reward. If you get to the front page, prepare for 50,000+ visitors.

High-Intent Directories

  • AlternativeTo: The "Holy Grail" of SEO. If someone searches for "Linktree Alternative," you want to be here.
  • SaaSHub: A massive directory that helps users compare software.
  • Peerlist: A professional network for builders. Think "LinkedIn for people who actually build things."

The "No-Face" Video Marketing Guide

You don't need a ring light or a studio. High-converting SaaS videos are often the simplest ones.

How to make a 30-second "Silent Demo":

  1. Screen Record: Use a tool like Loom or Screen Studio to record yourself using one feature (e.g., changing a Bio Theme).
  2. Add Text Overlays: Instead of speaking, add text like "No Credit Card Required" or "Set up in 60 seconds."
  3. Music over Voice: Use a trending (but professional) audio track.
  4. Call to Action: End with your URL. That's it.

The Full List of 14 Platforms

Here is the complete checklist of where to submit your SaaS today:

Reddit (r/SaaS)
Versily
Peerlist
BetaList
SaaSHub
TrustMRR
Dang AI
FounderList
AlternativeTo
MicroLaunch
Product Hunt
Indie Hackers
Startup Stash
Hacker News

Conclusion: Focus on What You Build

Marketing doesn't have to be a full-time job. By utilizing these free directories and organic SEO strategies, you can build a consistent stream of users while spending 90% of your time where you belong: In the code.
